title: Pertamina Geothermal and Toyota Partner to Launch Green Hydrogen Ecosystem in Indonesia
content: PT Pertamina Geothermal Energy Tbk (PGEO) and PT Toyota Motor Manufacturing Indonesia (TMMIN) have signed a joint declaration to develop a domestic green hydrogen ecosystem, leveraging Indonesiaâ€™s vast geothermal reserves. The agreement, signed on September 17 and announced on September 24, marks a strategic move to accelerate clean energy adoption in support of the countryâ€™s Net Zero Emissions 2060 target. The collaboration is a continuation of earlier renewable energy efforts between the two companies, including work on palm-based biodiesel, lubricants, and bioethanol. This new initiative expands PGEâ€™s â€œbeyond-electricityâ€ strategy by using geothermal heat not just for power generation, but also for producing hydrogen through energy-efficient electrolysis. This initiative builds on PGEâ€™s Ulubelu Green Hydrogen Pilot Project launched earlier in September, which serves as both a production facility and learning center for clean hydrogen. PGE is targeting 300MW of geothermal capacity to support green hydrogen production and aims to scale the ecosystem over the next five years. Julfi emphasized the need for â€œnew breakthroughsâ€ to accelerate green hydrogen development, citing long-term environmental benefits and industrial relevance. The collaboration with Toyota positions Indonesia to establish a homegrown hydrogen supply chain tied directly to its vehicle and energy sectors.
author: Analysts Account
